vision: Birkin is a personal agent OS — a memory layer and orchestrator sitting on top of multiple LLMs and external agents. Runs locally, scoped per-device, templates are shared but data belongs to the user.

north_star.token_efficiency: Minimize repeated context injection via memory compilation
north_star.work_automation: Cron + trigger-based scheduled execution, execution-to-judgment level action
north_star.personalized_evolution: Every LLM interaction compiles into the user's knowledge asset

ordering_principle: Dependency-driven. 2A.1 Multi-LLM + 2A.2 MCP (done) unlock the Graph Engine. Graph Engine unlocks Triggers and Budget. Memory Pipeline runs in parallel after Observability.

sprints.name: Phase 1 Debt Cleanup
sprints.goal: Fix review findings that block Phase 2 work

sprints.name: MCP Foundation (2A.2)
sprints.goal: First-class MCP client/server — Birkin can consume and expose MCP

sprints.name: Multi-LLM Connector Layer (2A.1)
sprints.goal: Unified connector layer calling multiple LLM providers through the same interface with capability metadata and routing

tasks.title: Provider capability metadata (ProviderProfile)
tasks.rationale: Foundation for model routing. Each provider declares capabilities (reasoning, search, code, vision), cost, latency tier, context window.
subtasks: Define Capability enum and ProviderProfile Pydantic model
Extend Provider ABC with profile property and supports() method
Add ProviderProfile to existing Anthropic and OpenAI providers
Add ProviderProfile to OpenRouter and CLI providers
acceptance_criteria: Every provider has a ProviderProfile with capabilities, cost, latency
provider.supports(Capability.REASONING) works correctly

tasks.title: New LLM providers (Perplexity, Gemini, Ollama, Groq)
tasks.rationale: Different tasks need different models. Perplexity for search, Gemini for multimodal, Ollama for fully local, Groq for low-latency.
subtasks: Tests: each provider completes with mock, profile correct
acceptance_criteria: 4 new providers implement Provider ABC
Each has correct ProviderProfile with capabilities

tasks.title: Provider routing (auto model selection)
tasks.rationale: Birkin routes to the right model based on task requirements. Manual override always available.
subtasks: ProviderRegistry: register all available providers, query by capability
ProviderRouter: select best provider given required capabilities + budget constraints
Fallback chain respects capabilities (don't fall back to a provider that can't do the task)
Config: default routing preferences in birkin_config.json
Tests: routing selects correct provider, fallback works
acceptance_criteria: Auto-routing selects provider based on capability match
Manual override bypasses routing
Fallback chain filters by capability

sprints.name: Graph Engine + Observability (2A.3 + 2A.8)
sprints.goal: State graph execution engine with structured observability logging

tasks.title: State graph execution engine (graph mode)
tasks.rationale: Mutable shared context (B model) + per-node checkpoints. Coexists with existing BFS simple mode. Enables conditional transitions, loops, parallel fan-out.
subtasks: GraphContext: mutable shared state with snapshot/restore
StateGraph: add_node, add_edge, add_conditional_edge, add_parallel, compile
CompiledGraph: ainvoke, astream, resume from checkpoint
LegacyNodeAdapter: wrap existing 30 nodes for graph mode
Tests: linear, branching, parallel, loop-with-guard, checkpoint/resume
acceptance_criteria: Graph mode supports conditional edges, parallel fan-out, loops with guard
Checkpoints persist to SQLite and resume correctly
Existing simple mode workflows unaffected
Legacy nodes usable in graph mode via adapter

tasks.title: Structured observability logging
tasks.rationale: Structured traces for every execution turn. Same data source for eval framework and dashboard. Trace → Span hierarchy.
subtasks: Define Trace and Span models (provider, tokens, latency, status)
Tests: trace emitted on execution, correct fields
acceptance_criteria: Every agent turn / graph node emits a structured span
Traces include provider, tokens, latency, tool calls
Traces queryable via API

sprints.name: Triggers + Token Budget (2A.5 + 2A.6)
sprints.goal: Workflows fire on triggers. Token budget enforces efficiency inline.

tasks.title: Trigger abstraction + Cron scheduler
tasks.rationale: Core automation primitive. Cron, file watch, webhook, message triggers fire workflows.
subtasks: WebhookTrigger: generic endpoint routing to workflows
TriggerScheduler: manages active triggers, lifespan integration
Tests: cron fires, file trigger detects change, webhook routes
acceptance_criteria: Cron trigger fires workflows on schedule
File watch detects changes
Webhook routes to correct workflow
Triggers persist across restart

tasks.title: Token Budget Manager
tasks.rationale: In-execution enforcement of token efficiency. Hard caps per node/workflow/session with auto-compress, downgrade, or stop.
subtasks: BudgetPolicy model: max tokens per node/workflow/session, cost caps
ContextCompressor: auto-summarize when budget is tight
Wire into GraphContext and provider call pipeline
Tests: budget exceeded triggers correct action, downgrade chain works
acceptance_criteria: Budget checks run before every provider call
Exceeding budget triggers compress/downgrade/stop per policy
Downgrade chain respects capability requirements

sprints.name: Memory Pipeline (2A.4 + 2B.3 + 2A.7)
sprints.goal: Raw event log → compiled knowledge. Semantic search over wiki. Evaluation framework.

tasks.title: Memory Compiler v1
tasks.rationale: Records every interaction as raw event, periodically compiles into structured wiki pages. Core of 'knowledge accumulation' vision.
subtasks: RawEvent model: session_id, event_type, provider, payload, tokens, outcome
Wiki structure extension: decisions/, patterns/, digests/
Tests: events recorded, compilation produces wiki pages
acceptance_criteria: Every LLM/tool call records a RawEvent
Compiler produces structured wiki pages from events
Scheduled compilation jobs work

tasks.title: Semantic memory (local embeddings)
tasks.rationale: BGE-m3 or sentence-transformers for bilingual KO+EN embedding. Semantic search over wiki. Token savings via accurate context retrieval.
subtasks: Auto-embed wiki pages on ingest, update on edit
Graceful fallback when model not downloaded
Tests: embed, store, retrieve, relevance ranking
acceptance_criteria: Wiki pages auto-embedded on ingest
Semantic search returns relevant results for KO and EN
Graceful fallback to keyword search

tasks.title: Evaluation framework
tasks.rationale: Compare responses across providers/workflows. Snapshot diff, latency, token/cost tracking. Evidence base for personalization.
subtasks: EvalRunner: run_case, run_dataset against provider/workflow targets
EvalResult: output, latency_ms, tokens, cost_usd, judge_score
Snapshot diff: compare current vs baseline, flag regressions
Tests: eval produces valid results, regression detection
acceptance_criteria: Eval runs produce JSONL with latency, tokens, cost
Snapshot diff detects regressions
CLI command works end-to-end

sprints.name: Agent Capabilities (2B.1 + 2B.2 + 2B.4 + 2B.5)
sprints.goal: Computer use, voice, context injection, approval gates — the agent does real work safely

tasks.title: Computer Use (Playwright MCP server)
tasks.rationale: Headless browser as MCP server. Unlocks 'monitor → summarize → notify' class of workflows.
subtasks: Create browser MCP server with Playwright backend
Tools: navigate, screenshot, click, type, extract, wait_for
Security: domain allowlist, max page load time, resource limits

tasks.title: Voice I/O (Whisper STT + TTS)
tasks.rationale: STT input + TTS output. Korean-language strength. WebUI mic + Telegram voice.
subtasks: WebUI: mic button in chat input
Tests: STT produces text, TTS produces audio

tasks.title: Context Injection Layer
tasks.rationale: Auto-inject relevant user context before agent calls via semantic search. Core of 'agent that knows me.'
subtasks: UserProfile: compiled from wiki, loaded at session start
Tests: relevant pages selected, context injected correctly

tasks.title: Approval Gates & Trust Boundaries
tasks.rationale: Safety boundary for execution-to-judgment level. Approval required before external effects.

sprints.name: UX & Intelligence (2C.1–2C.5)
sprints.goal: Command bar, fork/replay, dashboard, NL builder, insights — daily-use experience

tasks.title: Command Bar (natural language entry)
tasks.rationale: Raycast-style NL command bar. Routes to right workflow/agent. Removes context-switching cost.

tasks.title: Session Fork & Replay
tasks.rationale: Branch conversation, re-run from edited past turn. Built on Checkpointer from S3.

tasks.title: Observability Dashboard
tasks.rationale: Visualize trace/eval/budget data. Token spend, latency, error rate, cron history.

tasks.title: Natural Language Workflow Builder
tasks.rationale: Describe automation in a sentence → generate graph workflow. Entry point for non-developers.

tasks.title: Personal Insights Engine
tasks.rationale: Periodic insights from compiled memory. Weekly/monthly auto-reviews, pattern detection, goal progress.

dependency_map.critical_path: S0 → S1(MCP) → S2(Multi-LLM) → S3(Graph+Obs) → S4(Triggers+Budget) → S7(UX)
parallel_tracks: S5(Memory Pipeline) runs parallel to S4 after S3 completes
